form_semantics.justification: Every input has an associated <label> via for/id. All sections use <fieldset>/<legend>. Radio groups are nested in their own <fieldset> with a <legend>. Toggles use native checkbox inputs. File upload uses <input type='file'> with a label. Textarea has a label. No placeholder-only fields. autocomplete attribute used on display name.

element_choice.justification: 'Delete Account' is a <button type='button'> and 'Save Changes' is a <button type='submit'>. All interactive controls use native elements. No div-based interactive patterns. Correct use of <textarea>, <input type='file'>, and <input type='checkbox'> for toggles.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single <h1> ('My Account') in the header. A visually-hidden <h2> ('Account Settings') anchors the main content outline. Legends serve as section headings for each fieldset group — no redundant heading+legend duplication. No skipped levels.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA is used sparingly: <form> has aria-label='Account settings' which is appropriate since there's no visible heading directly labelling the form. No redundant role attributes. Skip link present. No aria-* used where native HTML suffices. tabindex='-1' on <main> is correct for skip-link target.

landmark_structure.justification: Skip link to #main-content present. <header>, <main> with id used correctly. Form is the primary content inside <main>. Page structure is simple and landmarks are not overused.

content_realism.justification: Controls have realistic labels and plausible names/values. However, inputs are empty with no example/default content, and the page lacks a realistic user context (e.g., pre-populated display name). Minor issue — structure handles variability well.

expectations_met: Radio button groups are within a <fieldset> with a <legend> describing the group
Toggle switches use checkbox inputs (not custom div patterns)
File upload uses <input type='file'> with a label
'Delete Account' is a <button> — not a link
Legends serve as visual headings — no redundant legend AND heading for same text
Radio buttons have matching name attributes within their group
The form as a whole is a <form> element
additional_observations: Nested fieldsets (for radio sub-groups inside outer fieldsets) use inline styles to remove default border/padding — functional but inline styles are minor style debt
autocomplete='nickname' on display name is a thoughtful accessibility/UX touch
Visually-hidden h2 provides a clean document outline without cluttering the visual UI
Skip link is correctly implemented and positioned as the first focusable element
